{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Vienna Game| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd| |nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | |bd| |pd| | | |= | | | | |pl| | | |= | | |nl| | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|= |rl| |bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} ==2...Bc5 == ==Anderssen Defence== This move allows White to immediately go after the bishop with [[/3. Na4|3. Na4?!]], but that allows Black to take advantage of the undefended a4-knight using the Hamppe-Meitner sacrifice [[/3. Na4/3...Bxf2|3...Bxf2+!]], named after the ''Immortal Draw'' (Hamppe-Meitner, Vienna 1872) Kxf2 Qh4+. It is premature to derail the knight on the rim like this when Black doesn't have any other piece out. The game continued: Kxf2 Qh4+ Ke3 Qf4+ Kd3 d5 Kc3 Qxe4 Kb3 Na6 a3 Qxa4+!! Kxa4 Nc5+ Kb4 a5+! Kxc5 Ne7 Bb5+ Kd8 Bc6 b6+ Kb5 Nxc6 Kxc6 Bb7+ Kb5 Ba6+ Kc6 Bb7+. 1/2-1/2http://www.chessgames.com/perl/chessgame?gid=1250160. The simple [[/3. Nf3|3. Nf3]] gives White a slight advantage, though.{{cite web|last=Goeller|first=Michael|publisher=Kenilworth Chess Club|url=http://www.kenilworthchessclub.org/games/java/2009/hamppe-meitner.htm|title=The Hamppe - Meitner Motif|accessdate=10 August 2015}} ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. 1. e4 e5 2.
